data.title: Coca-Cola and Procter & Gamble outperform on a bruising day overall for U.S. stocks
data.description:
Coca-Cola Company (KO) and Procter & Gamble (PG) outperformed on Tuesday amid a broad market decline tied to President Donald Trump's threat to impose new tariffs on European nations opposing the sale of Greenland to the United States. U.S. Treasury yields spiked, and the U.S. dollar fell as part of a flight to quality that also drew investors into defensive favorites Coca-Cola (KO) and Procter & Gamble (PG).

Coca-Cola (KO) closed with a 1.9% gain, while Procter & Gamble (PG) pushed 1.7% higher as the two consumer staples stocks stood on a day that 27 of the 30 Dow Jones Industrial Average constituents were down for the session. Procter & Gamble (PG) is due to report earnings on Thursday, while Coca-Cola (KO) steps into the earnings confessional during the second week of February.

Looking ahead, there is not expected to be a dramatic improvement in U.S.-European trade relations during the World Economic Forum in Davos this week, although analysts have pointed to the one-year track record of tariff threats being reeled in by President Trump within weeks and months of the initial threats as negotiations spin on.

data.title: SurgePays launches underwritten public offering of shares and warrants
data.description:
• SurgePays (SURG) said on Tuesday it has commenced an underwritten public offering of its common stock and/or pre-funded warrants.
  • The company said all securities in the offering will be sold by SurgePays and that the size and terms of the offering will depend on market conditions. 
  • SurgePays said the offering is being made pursuant to an existing shelf registration statement on file with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.
  • SURG -10.75% after hours to $1.66.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Netflix GAAP EPS of $0.56 beats by $0.01, revenue of $12.05B beats by $80M
data.description:
• Netflix (NFLX): Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.56 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $12.05B (+17.6% Y/Y) beats by $80M.
  • 1Q26 Revenue of $12.16B vs $12.17B consensus, EPS of $0.76 vs $0.81 consensus
  • For 2026, based on F/X rates as of 1/1/2026, we forecast revenue of $50.7B-$51.7B vs $50.98B consensus. This represents12%-14% year over year growth (or 11%-13% F/X neutral growth), driven by increases in membershipand pricing plus a projected rough doubling of ad revenue in 2026 vs. 2025.We’re targeting a 2026 operating margin of 31.5% (based on 1/1/26 F/X rates), up from 29.5% in 2025,which includes approximately $275M of acquisition-related expenses.
  • Press Release

data.title: Edison cut on Eaton Fire overhang at Wells Fargo; Public Service upgraded
data.description:
Edison International (EIX) -3.9% in Tuesday's trading as Wells Fargo downgraded the utility to Underweight from Equal Weight with a $59 price target, citing the Eaton Fire liability overhang, uncertainty around the California liability construct, and the state's regulatory environment moving forward.

Wells Fargo's Shahriar Pourreza said a full picture of the Eaton Fire investigation remains unclear, and liability estimates remain an overhang for Edison (EIX) in and of itself, and the analyst said he is awaiting more information on official cause and incremental news on the scale of claims to be paid as they get tallied up; the recent announcement of a lawsuit against Los Angeles County and several water agencies as cross plaintiffs is "a potential positive, but introduces complexity and longer timeline."

The other key outstanding piece of the Edison (EIX) story is the larger California wildfire liability
landscape, Pourreza wrote; while the company has mostly wrapped up its regulatory activity including the general rate case, legacy fire claims and cost of capital, the analyst still sees California as a high-risk environment entering a gubernatorial election year without an incumbent governor and as state wildfire liability remains an open question.

At the same time, Pourreza and his utilities team upgraded Public Service Enterprise Group (PEG) to Overweight from Equal Weight with a $92 PT, pointing to "a solid management team and fundamentals converging with attractive valuation and enhanced margin opportunity above the PTC levels in guidance."

Wells Fargo also downgraded Southern Co. (SO), OGE Energy (OGE), NorthWestern (NWE) and Idacorp (IDA) to Underperform from Equal Weight, all on valuation.

data.title: N.Y. budget doesn't raise taxes on millionaires as revenue outlook improves
data.description:
New York Governor Kathy Hochul said her proposed $260 billion state budget will not rely on higher income or corporate taxes, a move that may ease business concerns following the election of New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani, who campaigned on raising taxes for high earners and large companies.

Hochul said stronger-than-expected revenue allowed the state to expand spending without new broad-based taxes. Her budget proposal cites a resilient stock market, slower effects from federal tariffs, and rising wages and bonuses, which together are expected to generate about $9.5 billion in additional revenue over two years.

“The last four years we’ve proven something important, that you can make historic investments without raising income taxes, without mortgaging the next generation, without losing sight of what people can afford,” Hochul said during her budget address on Tuesday.

The governor has repeatedly pushed back on calls to raise taxes, including proposals from Mamdani to increase income tax rates by 2 percentage points on those earning more than $1 million annually and to lift corporate taxes by 4 percentage points. Hochul considered a corporate tax increase late last year to help cover potential budget gaps, including child care funding, but ultimately opted against the move.

Mamdani renewed his call for higher taxes following the budget’s release, saying it was time to “ask New York City’s wealthiest and large corporations to pay their fair share.” He said his administration is still reviewing the proposal.

Hochul’s plan does include expanded spending on child care, committing nearly $500 million this year and next to begin offering free care for 2-year-olds in New York City. The initiative aligns with part of Mamdani’s broader campaign promise to deliver universal free child care for young children.

While the budget avoids major tax increases, it proposes taxing nicotine pouches at the same rate as cigarettes, which are currently subject to a $5.35 per-pack state excise tax. The measure would apply to products such as Zyn, which is owned by Philip Morris International.

The plan would also decouple New York’s tax code from certain provisions in the 2025 federal tax law, a change the state budget office estimates would preserve $1.6 billion in revenue. Hochul also proposed eliminating state taxes on tips, mirroring recent federal policy changes, and safeguarding tax-exempt charitable donations from potential federal revisions.

data.title: U.S. clears potential $2.3B aircraft and torpedo sale to Singapore
data.description:
The U.S. State Department has authorized a possible foreign military sale to Singapore covering maritime patrol and reconnaissance aircraft, lightweight torpedoes and associated systems, the Pentagon said Tuesday.

The proposed package carries an estimated value of $2.3 billion.

The Pentagon said Boeing (BA) would serve as the lead contractor for the deal.

data.title: Netflix: What ratings and earnings surprise history say ahead of Q4 results
data.description:
As Netflix prepares to report its fourth quarter results, we look at what the company's ratings and earnings surprise history say.

Wall Street analysts are demonstrating strong confidence in the streaming giant. The stock currently carries an average analyst rating of 4.08, translating to a “Buy” recommendation.

Of the 45 analysts who have weighed in over the last 90 days, the sentiment skews decidedly bullish: 22 analysts rate the stock a Strong Buy, while another 8 have assigned a Buy rating. Meanwhile, 13 analysts maintain a Hold position, with just 1 Sell and 1 Strong Sell rating among the group.

Beyond traditional Wall Street coverage, Netflix also receives evaluation through Seeking Alpha’s proprietary rating systems. The platform offers a Quant Rating that employs factor-based analysis, weighting metrics according to their predictive value rather than simply averaging factor grades. The company has a "Hold" recommendation according to SA Quant Rating.

Additionally, Seeking Alpha’s own analysts provide independent ratings on the stock, who also recommend the stock as a "Hold" with a score of 3.39.

Earnings reports added volatility to the tale, with Q3'25 delivering a -15.66% EPS miss against expectations, contrasting earlier beats like Q1'25's 16.58% surprise. YoY growth flickered positively at 8.88% that quarter, but wild swings from -73% lows to 1,708% spikes revealed Netflix's unpredictable script.
